purpose: Freeze the initial PostFiat protocol premises before asking Qwen to answer high-level architecture, privacy, Cobalt, model-governance, monetary-policy, storage, RPC, and validator-governance questions.

core_context.project_identity: PostFiat is a Rust Layer 1 inspired by XRP's settlement and validator-list lessons, but it is not a rippled binary fork. It is intended to combine XRPL-style settlement ergonomics with Cobalt governance, post-quantum authorization, shielded privacy, and replayable model-assisted governance.
core_context.current_status: The current state is controlled pre-testnet engineering. Internal project-controlled validators, local harnesses, provider machines, replay packets, and docs are valid engineering evidence. No packet in this lane authorizes mainnet mutation, live validator-registry mutation, live Cobalt amendment submission, sidecar activation, or authority transfer.
current_architecture: Rust L1 implementation rather than a direct rippled fork.
Cobalt-style governance machinery for trust-graph and validator-registry transitions.
Qwen/SGLang TP=1 deterministic profile for internal constitutional replay, with governed model replacement required before mainnet authority.
Orchard/Halo2-style shielded flow currently implemented as the privacy baseline.
ML-DSA-style post-quantum authorization is the current authorization direction.
Fixed supply, fee burn, and no native validator rewards are current monetary-policy premises.
Validator evidence and UNL selection must be typed, hash-bound, replayable, challengeable, and Cobalt-gated before live authority.
non_goals: Do not treat Qwen prose as live authority.
Do not use hidden private reputation or unregistered social claims as constitutional evidence.
Do not let a model self-upgrade, expand its options, request secrets, or authorize live actions.
Do not claim public decentralization from project-controlled internal validation.
Do not silently substitute a prompt, model profile, evidence packet, selector, implementation mapping, or gate result after acceptance.
governance_principles: Every protocol question must use a closed option set.
Every answer must cite approved evidence fields or preserve, hold, or no-op.
Every accepted context, question, request, evidence packet, decision, and conformance mapping must be hash-bound.
Replay proves accountability and substitution resistance; it is not correctness by itself.
A bad replay-converged decision must remain challengeable through a typed challenge packet.
Cobalt or an explicit release gate must stand between model output and live protocol effect.
The founder or foundation can propose changes, but cannot silently override an accepted process without producing a detectable mismatch or a new governed amendment.

decision_queue.title: New Rust L1 versus full XRPL fork
decision_queue.question: Should PostFiat continue as a new Rust L1 rather than fully forking rippled/XRPL?
decision_queue.status_quo_summary: PostFiat is currently implemented as a Rust L1 with XRP-inspired transaction ergonomics, Cobalt governance, ML-DSA authorization, and Orchard/Halo2 privacy rather than as a direct rippled fork.

decision_queue.title: Orchard/Halo2 privacy versus attestation privacy
decision_queue.question: Should PostFiat preserve the Orchard/Halo2 shielded-flow baseline, replace it with Railgun-style attestation privacy, or adopt a hybrid disclosure layer?
decision_queue.status_quo_summary: The current privacy baseline is Orchard/Halo2-style shielded flow with deposit, spend, withdraw, scanning, and disclosure docs and tests.

decision_queue.title: Evidence-bound AI governance versus private committee judgment
decision_queue.question: Should PostFiat keep developing evidence-bound Qwen/Cobalt governance as the default validator and protocol-decision lane rather than relying on private foundation judgment?
decision_queue.status_quo_summary: The current governance direction is typed Qwen-generated evidence requests and decisions, deterministic replay, challenge packets, conformance mapping, and Cobalt or release-gate binding.

decision_queue.title: Cobalt hard governance gates
decision_queue.question: Which Cobalt trust-graph and validator-registry transition gates should remain constitutional hard requirements?
decision_queue.status_quo_summary: The current Cobalt baseline requires trust-graph linkedness, essential-subset threshold checks, validator-registry transition rules, stale replay rejection, dry-run no-mutation checks, and replay bundles before registry or trust-graph changes can become live authority.

decision_queue.title: ML-DSA authorization baseline
decision_queue.question: Should PostFiat preserve ML-DSA-style post-quantum authorization as a genesis protocol premise, alter the certificate/key-rotation design, or defer PQ authorization?
decision_queue.status_quo_summary: The current authorization direction is ML-DSA-style post-quantum signing and wallet authorization, with key-rotation and wallet implications documented.

decision_queue.title: Fixed supply and fee burn
decision_queue.question: Should PostFiat preserve fixed supply, fee burn, and no native validator rewards as constitutional monetary-policy invariants?
decision_queue.status_quo_summary: The current monetary-policy premise is fixed supply with fee burn and no native validator subsidy, while validator operation is justified by stakeholder utility rather than protocol inflation.
